issues.title: Kata Flight: add canonical kata JSON query helpers
issues.body:
# Problem

Kata Flight still pays repeated token and wall-clock costs when agents rediscover kata CLI JSON shapes and retry broken `jq` selectors.

Transcript evidence from the Claude corpus:

- `41a8f833...`: "`kata list` JSON shape isn't a flat array. Let me inspect it."
- `081654b3...`: "`kata list --json` shape isn't a flat array. Let me inspect it."
- `48012486...`: "The jq selectors are wrong — let me check kata's actual JSON shape."
- `cc91405d...`: "`kata list --json` is returning empty/non-array. Let me check the raw output shape."
- Additional Arc hits show `Cannot index number with string "labels"`, `Cannot index object with number`, and `Cannot index number with string "label"`.

Local evidence:

- `skills/kata-flight/SKILL.md` already documents the canonical shapes: `kata list --json` returns `{kata_api_version, issues:[...]}`, list labels are bare strings, `kata show --json` top-level labels are objects, and `.issue.labels` is null.

The prose warning is not enough. Agents still hand-write selectors.

# Recommendation

Add canonical query helpers or scripts for the common Kata Flight selectors and JSON reads.

Examples:

- list open ids by label
- list ready ids by label excluding `kind:rdr-seed`, `inbox:hold`, and `umbrella`
- show labels/owner/status for one issue
- extract top-level links and blockers
- count labels from `kata labels --json`

# Acceptance Criteria

- Add reusable helper commands/scripts or documented shell functions used by `kata-flight`, `kata-scope-review`, and related skills.
- Replace hand-written `jq` snippets in hot skill paths with helper calls or exact tested snippets.
- Add fixtures/tests for the three known kata JSON shapes.
- The docs explicitly forbid ad hoc `.[]` over `kata list --json`.
- The helper emits compact agent-readable output suitable for phase briefs.

# Priority

Priority 1. This is a repeated low-level retry cost and is easy to make deterministic.

# Related

- Related to `rdr#cbne` because both convert mechanical LLM-mediated checks into deterministic helpers.
- Related to `rdr#t31n` because retry counts should be measured.
issues.author: cwensel

issues.title: Kata Flight: add agentsview-backed flight cost report
issues.body:
# Problem

Kata Flight lacks a recurring cost report for flights, scope review, roborev refine, wakeups, and subagent handoff overhead. Agentsview already exposes useful primitives: token consumption, peak context, session duration, tools-per-turn, cache economics, and per-session usage.

The first report recommended a session-health loop. This second pass adds concrete metrics from transcript anti-patterns:

- stale wakeups
- JSON-shape retry failures
- subagent result retrieval fallbacks
- skill rereads
- worktree prep collisions
- roborev review cost
- scope-review fanout
- stop reasons

# Recommendation

Add a Kata Flight `_usage` recipe or script that uses agentsview plus kata/roborev metadata to produce a flight cost report.

# Acceptance Criteria

- Document commands for selecting Kata Flight sessions in agentsview.
- Report token use, wall-clock, peak context, tool calls, subagent count, and wakeup count.
- Include Kata Flight-specific metrics: waves, shipped/stopped/skipped, scope-review fanout, roborev iterations, auto-resume count, worktree prep failures.
- Include retry metrics for kata JSON-shape failures and permission/sandbox failures where detectable.
- Output a JSON file suitable for trend comparison.

# Priority

Priority 1. Measurement is the feedback loop for deciding which cost fixes matter.

# Related

- Related to `rdr#t31n` engine-level agentsview cost dashboard.
issues.author: cwensel

issues.title: Kata Flight: add budgeted tier-0 scope-review triage
issues.body:
# Problem

Scope review prevents costly flight flapping, but full grounding can itself be expensive. Transcript evidence shows:

- `24630869...`: scope-review was introduced because `marquez-import` spent day-plus time never draining.
- `d3a90283...`: a review grounded 7 katas in corpus/RDRs/feature matrix and reproduced HEAD behavior.
- `2031bcd8...`: a smaller review merged duplicates and embedded plans.

The gate is useful. The cost leak is running full grounding where cheap signals could decide "skip, already reviewed, simple in-scope, or needs full review."

# Recommendation

Add a tier-0 scope-review triage pass before full per-kata grounding.

Tier-0 should use cheap tracker/source signals:

- already `lifecycle:reviewed`
- `kind:*`, `inbox:*`, `umbrella`
- duplicate title/body candidates
- explicit RDR-shaped language
- missing cited code locus
- seam-accretion count threshold
- recently closed/fixed same-locus issues

# Acceptance Criteria

- Define tier-0 output categories: `skip-reviewed`, `full-ground`, `likely-dupe`, `likely-rdr-shaped`, `hold`, `simple-in-scope`.
- Full grounding runs only for `full-ground` and uncertain categories.
- The report records how many katas were avoided by tier-0.
- Conservative bias remains: uncertain work goes to full review or RDR seed, not silent close.
- Add a measurement hook for scope-review fanout and wall-clock duration.

# Priority

Priority 1. Scope review should stay, but broad full grounding should be budgeted.

# Related

- Related to `rdr#bjxn` budgeted review outputs.
- Related to `rdr#t31n` measurement.
issues.author: cwensel

issues.title: Kata Flight: measure and route roborev refine cost
issues.body:
# Problem

Kata Flight keeps roborev refine as a core quality gate. That is correct, but review cost is not currently first-class. Transcript evidence shows even clean review can be expensive:

- `18c57e67...`: "Review passed on first iteration. No fixes needed. Refine complete." with `total_tokens: 80202` and `tool_uses: 46`.
- Existing workflow text describes a `fix -> commit -> close-old-review -> re-review` loop.
- `roborev-triage` exists because the RDR launch path learned that refine loops can flap.

The issue is not "remove roborev." It is that the process needs to know when refine is expensive, flapping, or low-yield.

# Recommendation

Track and route roborev refine cost per kata.

Metrics:

- review jobs seen
- iteration count
- token/tool count when available
- wall-clock duration
- findings fixed/dropped/pushed
- first-pass clean but high-cost cases
- max-iteration stops

Use the metrics to decide whether to continue refine, switch to one-pass triage, or stop with a structured cost/flap reason.

# Acceptance Criteria

- `kata-ship` records roborev iteration count and disposition counts.
- `kata-flight` final report can summarize high-cost refine cases compactly.
- Add structured stop labels/events for max iterations and daemon/refine flaps.
- Define a threshold for "first-pass clean but high-cost" reporting.
- Do not weaken the normal review gate without an explicit policy.

# Priority

Priority 2. Quality gates stay intact, but measurement enables cost-aware routing.

# Related

- Related to `rdr#t31n` measurement.
- Related to `rdr#ebc5` only where waiting/polling contributes to review cost.
issues.author: cwensel

issues.title: Kata Flight: add worktree debris preflight classifier
issues.body:
# Problem

Worktree and branch debris still causes recovery loops.

Transcript evidence:

- `319f0edd...`: `fatal: a branch named 'kata/40t4' already exists`.
- `7b6d5a0a...`: `fatal: '.worktrees/kata-40t4' already exists`.
- `98eee37f...`: preflight fails because the working tree has uncommitted changes and branch is not main.
- `6872b50f...`: resolve agent reused an existing checkout instead of creating an isolated worktree, then had to recover.

Kata Flight has good invariants once it reaches the worktree phase, but debris classification still happens too late.

# Recommendation

Add a cheap debris classifier before worktree creation and before `--resume` adoption.

Classifier outcomes:

- clean: create worktree
- live session: halt/surface owner
- dead lock: offer reclaim
- stale branch only: cleanup/human choice
- stale path only: cleanup/human choice
- primary dirty/off-branch: refuse before claiming

# Acceptance Criteria

- Add the classifier to `kata-flight` and `kata-ship` prep flow.
- It checks branch, path, owner, lifecycle label, and primary checkout state before mutation.
- It reports one structured `stopped:*` or `reclaimable:*` code.
- It avoids attempting `git worktree add` when branch/path collision is already known.
- Include examples for branch-only, path-only, both-present, and dead-lock cases.

# Priority

Priority 1. Prevents repeated setup failures and reduces risk around cleanup.

# Related

- Related to `rdr#dekn` only conceptually: both reduce resume/recovery rehydration.
issues.author: cwensel

issues.title: Kata Flight: preclassify external daemon and tracker writes
issues.body:
# Problem

Kata Flight frequently needs operations outside pure workspace reads: kata daemon access, roborev daemon checks, tracker mutations, and classifier-governed writes. Transcript evidence shows predictable first-attempt failures:

- `bed2b483...`: sandbox restriction blocks kata access, then rerun with sandbox disabled.
- `ff9fac41...`: roborev daemon not running and kata daemon cannot bind.
- `8a9779bc...`: permission denied posting a kata comment as an external-system write.
- `1f7980b3...`: classifier denied autonomous kata creation; protocol required surfacing `KATA_PUSH`.

These failures cost turns and can leave state half-classified.

# Recommendation

Add a preflight classification table for operations that require escalation, daemon health, or parent-owned mutation. Skills should request the right execution mode up front or emit a packet for the parent to perform the mutation.

# Acceptance Criteria

- Document which operations require external daemon/tracker access.
- Add preflight checks for kata daemon, roborev status, and known classifier-restricted mutations.
- Leaf agents never attempt parent-owned tracker mutations; they emit packets.
- A sandbox/permission denial is retried at most once after classification.
- Repeated denial becomes `stopped:permission-boundary` or another structured reason.

# Priority

Priority 1. The fix removes predictable failed attempts and makes boundary handling auditable.

# Related

- Related to `rdr#t31n` because permission/retry counts should be measured.
issues.author: cwensel

issues.title: Kata Flight: split hot prompts from cold rationale
issues.body:
# Problem

Kata Flight skills mix operative rules, historical rationale, incident explanations, and examples in the same hot prompt surface. Claude transcript evidence shows repeated reads of full skill files:

- `509cd0b6...`: read of plugin-cache `kata-flight/SKILL.md`.
- `2be26a24...`: read of `.claude/skills/kata-flight/SKILL.md`.
- `bf5ed090...`: repeated offset read of the same `SKILL.md`.
- `13bee859...`: tool said the file state was current and no readback was needed, yet later sessions reread skill state.

External support:

- OpenAI prompt caching guidance rewards stable static prefixes and variable data at the end.
- Anthropic context-engineering guidance treats context as a managed, finite resource.

# Recommendation

Split the largest Kata Flight skills into:

- hot operative prompt: exact gates, commands, packet schemas, output contracts;
- cold rationale/reference: incident history, why alternatives failed, extended examples.

The hot prompt should cite cold anchors but not carry the full rationale into every run.

# Acceptance Criteria

- Pilot the split on `kata-flight` and `worktree-ship-pipeline` or another high-traffic pair.
- Hot prompt contains only required execution rules and links/cites cold sections.
- Cold rationale remains available for maintainers and unusual failures.
- Run a small before/after token estimate or agentsview comparison.
- Ensure no safety invariant is removed; move rationale, do not delete gates.

# Priority

Priority 1. This reduces repeated context load without weakening process behavior.

# Related

- Related to `rdr#pmnf` template conditionality.
- Related to `rdr#t31n` measurement.
issues.author: cwensel
